diagnosis: Fish hook impaction in the esophagus
symptom: something that snagged going down and a pain high behind the breastbone that will not ease
hook: a small curved piece of metal was caught sideways in the food pipe with its barb dug into the wall
whatYouSee: A small curved metal hook with its barb caught sideways in the food pipe.
whyItMatters: The barb that is built to hold a fish in place holds just as well in soft human tissue and will not slide back out on its own.
treatment: It comes out with an endoscope guided down the food pipe so the barb can be freed without tearing tissue.
takeaway: A hook does not care what it catches.
igOptions: A. A fish bone stuck in the throat
B. A fish hook lodged in the esophagus

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with something that snagged going down and a pain high behind the breastbone that will not ease.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And a small curved piece of metal was caught sideways in the food pipe with its barb dug into the wall.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Fish hook impaction in the esophagus

👀 What you see:
A small curved metal hook with its barb caught sideways in the food pipe.

💊 Treatment:
It comes out with an endoscope guided down the food pipe so the barb can be freed without tearing tissue.

🦴 Why it matters:
The barb that is built to hold a fish in place holds just as well in soft human tissue and will not slide back out on its own.
